briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 8, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Direct Impacts
sections.content: According to reports today, six extreme heatwaves that occurred between 2003 and 2024 were potentially fatal when considering temperature and humidity, and have already resulted in thousands of deaths. New research warns that extreme heat has reached 'unsurvivable' levels for humans [59]. Indeed, the past three years have been the hottest on record, with global emissions still at peak levels, and the planet continuously approaching the 1.5°C warming limit [60]. Despite water conservation efforts, climate change is outpacing these efforts in areas such as Phoenix, Denver, and Las Vegas, highlighting the severity of water scarcity [33, 51, 52]. Furthermore, climate change poses widespread threats across entire ecosystems, endangering the safety of student athletes and birds, and, in conjunction with overfishing, affecting fish size reduction and shellfish [37, 38, 49, 53].

sections.title: ⚠️ Paradox of Climate Policy and Geopolitical Instability
sections.content: Internationally, significant resistance and controversy regarding climate change response policies are being observed. The Administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has been criticized for praising groups opposing government action on climate change and advocating for the repeal of climate change policies [34, 42]. In particular, the EPA Administrator's keynote address at an event hosted by a climate change-denying think tank deepens concerns [58]. In Oklahoma, legislation is being advanced to block climate change-related lawsuits against fossil fuel companies, and the Chesapeake Bay Foundation is joining a lawsuit against the relaxation of federal greenhouse gas regulations, indicating an intensification of legal and policy conflicts [41, 54]. Meanwhile, within a month of the outbreak of the Iran war, at least 60 countries responded to a global energy crisis, revealing the complex impact of geopolitical instability on the climate crisis, including rising food prices [61, 62]. Amidst these circumstances, India's withdrawal of its bid to host the COP33 climate talks has also raised concerns about the direction of international climate cooperation [36].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Expansion Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, local governments and businesses continue their efforts to achieve carbon neutrality. Goyang City has initiated a carbon neutrality implementation review, and Gwangmyeong City has established a five-year roadmap to accelerate the creation of a carbon-neutral environmental education city [11, 14, 16]. Woori Bank has established a new ESG strategy, 'NEXT ESG,' to accelerate carbon neutrality practices [12], and urban agriculture has been presented as a practical platform connecting carbon neutrality and community restoration [13]. In the renewable energy sector, the South Chungcheong Provincial Council is focusing on establishing a regional foundation, including passing an ordinance to expand resident-participatory renewable energy and promoting new models [18, 24, 25, 27]. The Han River Basin Environmental Office has initiated full-scale renewable energy cooperation by holding the Seoul Metropolitan Area Climate and Energy Policy Consultative Body [17, 29], and news has also been reported that a new era has begun as renewable energy generation capacity has reached 100GW [21]. Furthermore, demand for electric vehicle conversion is increasing, with the Seoul Metropolitan Government's first-half budget for electric cargo truck subsidies being fully depleted due to high oil prices [32]. Technological innovations such as the development of new hybrid solar panels capable of generating electricity even on rainy days, KCL's construction of a 2MW solar power generation facility [28, 31], and Hanwha's confirmed commitment to fostering new and renewable energy [26] are interpreted as positive signals.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Action and Cultural Responses
sections.content: In the international community, various efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals have been reported. China Carbon Neutral Development has signed a green hydrogen agreement, and Aston University has made significant progress towards achieving its net-zero targets [56, 57]. Canada TBM is supporting 21 youth climate change projects through subsidies, encouraging the participation of future generations [55]. Meanwhile, there have also been notable reports on the impact of climate change on human cultural experiences. Research indicating that the Earth's diverse smells are disappearing suggests widespread cultural loss [35]. Furthermore, a study showing that ancient climate change influenced the formation of diversity in African frogs and that its effects persist to this day highlights the long-term ecological impacts of climate change [45]. Efforts to deliver climate information to specific communities are also continuing, such as the launch of 'Frío, Tibio, Caliente,' a climate change podcast for Latinx communities in the U.S. [48].

briefing.closing: Despite the severity of climate threats reported today and some policy setbacks, we have confirmed that substantial efforts to achieve carbon neutrality and expand renewable energy are continuing both domestically and internationally. Emphasizing once again the importance of international cooperation and swift action to address climate change, this concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: An electric vehicle enters the Seoul Government Complex in Jongno-gu, Seoul, where the five-day vehicle restriction system was in effect on the 24th of last month. Yonhap News. Due to high oil prices combined with the effect of new car releases, Seoul City's first-half budget for electric vehicle subsidies for commercial trucks has been fully depleted. The city believes that the increase in subsidy applications is not unrelated to the recent high oil price situation and is considering a supplementary budget to secure additional funds. According to the city on the 8th, this year's electric commercial vehicle subsidy applications...

summary.phenomenon: Seoul City's first-half budget for electric commercial vehicle subsidies has been fully depleted.
summary.cause: This is due to a surge in electric vehicle subsidy applications, driven by recent high oil prices and the effect of new car releases.
summary.outlook: Seoul City is considering forming a supplementary budget to secure additional funds in response to the increased subsidy applications.

articles.title: ‘Non-survivable’: heatwaves are already breaching human limits, with worse to come, study finds
articles.description: Analysis of six extreme heatwaves found when temperature and humidity were accounted for, all were potentially deadly for older peopleExtreme heat is already creating “non-survivable” conditions for humans in heatwaves that have killed thousands and likely many more, according to new research that warns people are more susceptible to rising temperatures than first thought.Scientists re-examined six extreme heatwaves between 2003 and 2024 and found that when temperature, humidity and the body’s a

summary.phenomenon: An analysis of six extreme heatwaves between 2003 and 2024 found that, when temperature and humidity were considered, all were potentially deadly, especially for older people, and have already caused thousands of deaths. According to new research, extreme heat is creating 'non-survivable' conditions for humans.
summary.cause: New research indicates that people are more vulnerable to rising temperatures than previously thought, and the combination of temperature and humidity is creating non-survivable conditions for humans.
summary.outlook: The study warns that 'worse is yet to come,' projecting that non-survivable conditions and fatalities due to extreme heatwaves will intensify further in the future.

articles.original_title: Are OnlyFans models the best way to explain the climate crisis?
articles.description: Actor Megan Prescott has joined with Adam McKay in the hope that showing bite-size web videos of women undressing will persuade us to save the world. Will it work?The world, as we know, is in trouble. The last three years have been the hottest ever recorded. Global emissions are still at record highs. The planet is now consistently flirting with the 1.5C limit it promised not to cross. Increasingly, it feels as if we need a genuine miracle to stop us from sleepwalking into catastrophe. Could tha

summary.phenomenon: The past three years have been the hottest on record, and global emissions remain at all-time highs. The planet is consistently approaching the 1.5°C warming limit.
summary.outlook: The planet is described as 'sleepwalking into catastrophe,' and severe future impacts are anticipated if current trends continue.
